episodes.title: Three Epstein survivors on what the files aren’t telling you
episodes.description:
It’s been more than a month since the Justice Department released the latest tranche of files related to its investigation of Jeffrey Epstein — around 3 million in total — yet the fallout shows no sign of slowing down. 

The release almost certainly wouldn’t have happened if it weren’t for a group of survivors who’ve been fighting for transparency and accountability. In this episode, Kara sits down with three women who've been part of that fight: Dani Bensky, Jess Michaels and Liz Stein. Kara, 

Dani, Jess and Liz talk about how Epstein’s survivors came together after decades of being siloed, who’s helping them fight for justice, and who’s standing in the way. They also talk about what the files reveal about the “Epstein Class” and what it says about how power works in this country. 

00:00 Introduction 
03:00 How each survivor was pulled into Epstein’s orbit
05:00 Why they chose to speak publicly
12:50 The physical and emotional cost of trauma
19:04 The Epstein files and DOJ controversy
23:00 Chaos, redactions, and intimidation
31:30 Reaction to the Pam Bondi hearing
35:30 Is this political?
42:42 Who is pushing for accountability?
48:00 Why survivors shouldn’t name co-conspirators
51:30 How power protects power
55:52 Has Donald Trump been exonerated?
58:45 Virginia’s law and statute of limitations
1:03:00 How advocacy helps healing
